titleStmt.title: Memorandum of Conversation, by the Acting Secretary of State

head.hi: Memorandum of Conversation, by the Acting Secretary of State

div.p:
The Minister of Finland called to see me this morning at his request. The Minister left with me an aide-mémoire Not printed. giving a detailed account
of the hostile acts committed by Russia within Finnish territory during the past few days.

The Minister then read to me, by instruction of his Government, a statement of the policy of the Finnish Government as already communicated by the Finnish Minister in LondonG. A. Gripenberg. to the British Government. The statement contained the following declaration:
“The Government of Finland desires to maintain unaltered its relations with Great Britain. The Government of Finland is a co-belligerent with Germany solely against Russia.”

The Minister asked if I would make any statement as to the attitude of this Government with regard to Finnish involvement in the war. I replied that I should prefer to await developments for at least a few days before making any such statement, but that the declaration as communicated to me by the Minister would, of course, facilitate the determination which this Government would reach.
